Check 12 fail: labeled 'medium' but four high-severity interacting issues are present and the tile-level split leakage specific to computational pathology (neighboring tiles from the same slide are near-duplicates) requires domain knowledge about spatial correlation in histopathology that is beyond general ML intuition. Expected failure on IDR and DC for a non-specialist single-pass assessor. Recommend restoring difficulty to 'hard'. Check 7 N/A (critique case, not defense_wins).

Check 12 fail: labeled 'medium' but the critical-slot WER failure mode (rare address tokens and medication names dominate dispatch risk even when mean WER improves) requires NLP evaluation knowledge specific to emergency dispatch ASR, and the latency-dispatch-action interaction requires understanding real-time systems constraints in 911 operations. Expected failure on DRQ and IDR dimensions for a general ML reviewer. Recommend restoring difficulty to 'hard'. Check 7 N/A (critique case).

Check 12 fail: labeled 'medium' but the ground_truth_rationale explicitly states 'this hard case requires non-ML battery expertise,' and the notes state 'substantial non-ML domain knowledge requirements.' The coin-cell-to-pack generalization failure requires knowing that EV packs operate under thermal gradients, fast-charge cycles, mechanical compression, and pack-level BMS dynamics, and that Li-metal safety claims require abuse testing beyond cycle-life metrics. A general ML reviewer cannot articulate the corrective empirical test (pouch-cell plus pack-level abuse protocols) without battery domain knowledge. Expected failure on IDR and ETD for non-specialists. The internal relabeling rationale conflicts with the stated expertise requirement. Recommend restoring difficulty to 'hard'. Check 7 N/A (critique case).

REVISED HARD CASE. Check 7 N/A (mixed case, not defense_wins). Cross-paragraph inference confirmed: endpoint change requires linking baseline appendix (24-hour severe-sepsis screening) to transformer optimization target (12-hour vasopressor escalation) across separate background and methods paragraphs; timestamp mismatch requires linking the protocol mention of EHR-charted timestamps to the data-quality note about specimen-time indexing; patient-overlap requires connecting the recurring ICU admission observation to the encounter-level random split; cohort construction change requires connecting the legacy ADT feed description to the care-episode service description. Red herrings appear naturally: lactate and vasopressor features before prediction time are explicitly described as valid; 1,126-episode external cohort with 80% power is explicitly described as valid. Check 12 PASS: expected failure on at least 2 rubric dimensions without cross-paragraph synthesis and clinical informatics knowledge.

REVISED HARD CASE. Check 7 N/A (mixed case). Cross-paragraph inference confirmed: objective change implicit by comparing renewal-scorecard historical tables to margin-reporting challenger across separate paragraphs; DR nuisance fitting on full four-quarter log while Q3-Q4 is the reporting period requires linking methodology and reporting-window paragraphs; Q3-Q4 operating cutoff tuning stated in separate deployment paragraph; frozen incumbent calibration stated in historical context paragraph separate from fresh Bayesian tuning. Red herrings appear naturally: Finance-approved margin metric sounds rigorous; regional propensity variation (0.15-0.62) is a tempting DR validity attack. Check 12 PASS.

REVISED HARD CASE. Check 7 N/A (critique case). Cross-paragraph inference confirmed: settlement-feed baseline vs authorization-stream challenger requires linking operations and pipeline paragraphs; full-stream vs settled-only label maturation requires linking label-operations background to label-quality governance paragraph; Q4 threshold calibration and Q4 reporting appear in separate paragraphs; model-plus-pipeline bundling is only visible by combining migration description with historical baseline context. Red herrings appear naturally: PR-AUC for imbalanced fraud is standard; 500-merge entity-resolution audit is well-documented. Check 12 PASS.

REVISED HARD CASE. Check 7 N/A (mixed case). Cross-paragraph inference confirmed: aggregate mAP headline vs nighttime safety claim are in different paragraphs; 38ms latency is buried in the systems engineering paragraph separate from the headline detector metrics; planner 400ms persistence requirement appears only in the final paragraph. Red herrings appear naturally: identical camera hardware and annotation workflow tempts reviewer to accept controlled comparison; Euro NCAP-compliant 1,847-frame nighttime slice sounds rigorous. Check 12 PASS: connecting per-frame COCO-style mAP to the planner's 400ms temporal window requires ADAS domain knowledge about detector-to-planner pipeline timing.

REVISED HARD CASE. Check 7 N/A (mixed case). Check 12 partial fail: cross-paragraph inference is achieved — NDCG headline vs watch-time objective, catalog-expansion historical precedent vs ranking-only change, and replay logs vs two-stage architecture are each distributed across separate paragraphs. However, all three must-find issues are medium severity, and a careful single-pass assessor familiar with recommender systems would likely catch NDCG as a watch-time proxy (check 3 direct) and replay limitations (broadly known). The catalog-expansion distinction is the only genuinely hard inference step. Expected failure on IDP but not clearly on two independent rubric dimensions. Recommend adding one high-severity must-find issue (e.g., that IPW estimator trained on logging policy from current retrieve-then-rank system cannot correctly reweight a ranker operating on the same candidate pool without retrieval independence assumptions) to ensure two-dimension failure.

REVISED HARD CASE. Check 7 N/A (mixed case). Cross-paragraph inference confirmed: finding-label F1 setup in benchmark paragraph vs full-report deployment recommendation in committee paragraph; edit-distance statistic in implementation appendix paragraph separate from the workflow-burden claim in the recommendation paragraph; missing time-to-edit study is only detectable by surveying what the packet does not measure across the whole memo. Red herrings appear naturally: kappa = 0.71 is a plausible annotator agreement standard; F1 = 0.91 with a narrow CI looks statistically strong; qualitative radiologist feedback is genuinely positive. Check 12 PASS: all three must-find issues require linking non-adjacent paragraphs and understanding the distinction between finding-label benchmarking and full-report quality in radiology workflow.

REVISED HARD CASE. Check 7 N/A (mixed case). Cross-paragraph inference confirmed: weather-regime mismatch requires comparing incumbent validation window (paragraphs 2-3: record cold snap, above-average peaks) with challenger evaluation window (paragraph 4: post-reliability stabilization); outage-regime difference requires linking the 23-outage incumbent period to the 7-outage challenger period across different paragraphs; forecast cutoff difference requires linking scheduling-process context (prior-day 16:00) to challenger methodology (06:00 next morning). Red herrings appear naturally: NERC forced-outage exclusion is an industry standard that tempts challenge as improper; NWS weather inputs shared by both systems may look like an equalizer. Check 12 PASS: utility load-forecasting domain knowledge required to recognize that weather regime, outage environment, and forecast information timing all matter to MAPE comparability.

REVISED HARD CASE. Check 7 N/A (mixed case). Cross-paragraph inference confirmed: self-selection requires linking graduation-cycle context paragraph to opt-in active-search rollout paragraph; seasonal confounding requires connecting Feb-May window to graduation-cycle seasonality statement; integration selection requires connecting ATS-enterprise-large-account context to the integration-tier lift result; regional timing requires linking staggered enablement dates to aggregate reporting. Red herrings appear naturally: 48-hour label-maturation lag is a legitimate data hygiene practice; interview-to-application rate is a board-approved KPI. Check 12 PASS: four issues each require cross-paragraph synthesis; no single paragraph reveals any flaw directly.

REVISED HARD CASE. Check 7 N/A (critique case). Cross-paragraph inference confirmed: year-end close seasonality requires connecting accounting-environment paragraph (accruals/reserves concentrated at annual close) to Q4 precision result; dual-approval policy change requires linking post-Q2 controls paragraph to second-half comparison period; model-influenced labels requires combining escalation-definition paragraph with the 100%-review-of-flagged statement and the sampling note for non-flagged activity; no same-period holdout is only visible by noticing Q1-Q2 vs Q3-Q4 structural asymmetry. Red herrings appear naturally: quarter-end volume spikes are described as normal accounting operations; manual accruals and reserves are described as routine close activity. Check 12 PASS.

REVISED HARD CASE. Check 7 N/A (mixed case). Check 12 partial fail: only two must-find issues are present (merchant_category_scope_slippage and issuer_label_policy_dependence). Cross-paragraph inference is required for both, but with only two issues, a single-pass assessor who finds the scope slippage in the final paragraph (where the rollout language is stated relatively explicitly) may fail only one rubric dimension. The issuer-label-policy dependence issue is genuinely subtle and requires domain knowledge about how dispute-resolution pipelines produce fraud labels differently across issuers. However, the scope-slippage issue may be partially visible within the final paragraph alone ('travel bookings, digital goods, and subscription services' vs. e-commerce training). Recommend adding a third must-find issue — for example, that the secondary partner-bank validation was not pre-specified as a primary calibration source and therefore cannot confirm label-regime equivalence — to ensure two-dimension failure for a single-pass assessor.
